positives: It recognizes and avoids obstacles like cables and pet toys, eliminating the need to pre-clean before each run.
Hard floor cleaning is excellent.
The mopping capability actually lifts dried stains rather than just pushing water around like budget models.
The dock handles most everyday maintenance on its own; you mostly just have to refill water and empty the dustbin occasionally.
It automatically heats water to sanitize mop pads, so you’re not spreading dirty water around.
Its slim design allows it to fit under low furniture, preventing dust buildup in hard-to-reach spots.
negatives: Disappointing pet hair pickup on carpet.
Struggles to pull embedded fur from deep carpet fibers.

positives: Outperforms the Saros when it comes to pet hair pickup on carpets.
Does a great job on bare floors.
The Tri-Cut brushroll features rotating blades that cut through hair to prevent tangling, improving pet hair pickup.
The multifunction dock empties the dustbin, refills the water tank, and washes the mop pads with hot water and air, handling nearly all day-to-day maintenance.
negatives: Obstacle avoidance is a step down from the Roborock Saros 10R, so a quick sweep of the floors is recommended before running it.
Performance is inconsistent when recognizing and avoiding pet waste, making it best to run while home.
The onboard dustbin is small, requiring frequent returns to the dock to self-empty in larger homes or when cleaning intervals are extended.

positives: Picks up nearly everything on hard floors in one pass, performing almost as well as the pricier MOVA P50 Pro Ultra.
Obstacle handling is strong thanks to multiple LiDAR sensors, preventing frequent collisions with furniture legs.
The 3-in-1 design converts into a stick or handheld vacuum for spot-cleaning stairs or car interiors.
The self-emptying dock automates maintenance tasks.
negatives: The smaller bag in the self-emptying dock fills quickly in larger homes, increasing emptying frequency and ownership costs.

positives: Often heavily discounted to around $200, making it very competitive at that price point.
Among the best cheap options tested by RTINGS.
negatives: At full retail price, it lacks features compared to many mid-range options, making it a hard sell.

positives: It delivered flawless obstacle avoidance in our evaluations, earning a perfect 24/24 score in lab testing.
At just 3.14 inches tall, its StarSight Autonomous System 2.0 uses time-of-flight sensing and an RGB camera to reliably detect and weave around cables, toys, and low-lying furniture.
VertiBeam lateral-beam technology and hyper-responsive path planning ensure it sidesteps obstacles without hesitation.
HyperForce suction and a zero-tangle brush roll tackle debris across all floor types.
Its AdaptiLift chassis mounts thresholds up to 4 cm and lifts the mop on carpets.
The 10-in-one dock handles dust collection, mop washing, heated drying, and more.
It delivers up to 30,000 Pa of suction power for strong debris pickup on hard floors and carpets.
Its OZMO Roller 3.0 instant self-washing mopping system continuously cleans the roller while mopping for more consistent stain removal compared with traditional pads.
AI-enhanced sensors and intelligent path planning identify obstacles and clean efficiently around furniture.
TruEdge edge-cleaning technology reaches corners and walls.
The Omni station provides fully automated maintenance with auto dustbin emptying, hot-water mop washing, water refilling, and hot-air drying.
It can cover homes up to roughly 500 m² in a single cleaning cycle.
It features BLAST suction combined with ZeroTangle 4.0 anti-tangle technology for consistent debris pickup.
The upgraded OZMO Roller 3.0 system uses a ~26–27 cm microfiber roller with pressurized self-washing via up to 32 water nozzles for reduced residual moisture and streak-free floors.
FocusJet stain pre-dissolving technology uses high-pressure crossed water jets (around 46,000 Pa) and infrared-based stain detection for targeted cleaning.
Its TruePass adaptive 4-wheel drive system and AIVI 3D 4.0 obstacle avoidance deliver reliable navigation.
The OmniCyclone dock includes a bagless PureCyclone 2.0 auto-empty system, a 1.6 L dustbin, and fully automated mop washing, drying, and maintenance for up to 48 days of hands-free use.
It boasts 20,000 Pa turbo suction capable of deep-cleaning carpets and hard floors.
The HydroJet System continuously refreshes the mop with dual water reservoirs and a dual-scraper structure that cleans the roller 360 times per minute.
Includes a detachable FlexiOne portable deep cleaner for fabric surfaces and carpets.
DuoSpiral detangle brushes minimize hair tangling and maintenance.
Its all-in-one station offers self-emptying, self-cleaning, hot-air drying, automatic detergent dispensing, wastewater collection, and self-refilling.
It recognizes over 30 stain types and supports Matter protocol compatibility.
Dual HD cameras and dual-chip AI identify and avoid over 120 obstacle types.
Its 12,000 Pa suction handles dirt and pet hair effectively.
EdgeSwing cleans along walls and a baseboard-cleaning module tackles baseboard dust.
Dual rotating mop heads apply 1.2 kg force at 180 RPM to remove stubborn stains.
The docking station self-cleans and dries mops hygienically for up to 120 days.

records.recommendation_type: Best Budget Robot Vacuum for Mid-2025
positives: It pairs an OZMO Mega Mopping System with a high-frequency vibrating mop head and electrically controlled water delivery.
Equipped with an 8,000 Pa high-speed motor that effortlessly lifts dirt and debris from hard floors and carpets.
Its PureCyclone 4-layer, 14-cyclone filtration captures fine dust in seconds.
ZeroTangle Anti-Tangle Technology prevents pet hair and fibers from clogging brushes.
TrueMapping Tech with 360° LiDAR scanning ensures fast, precise room mapping and efficient path planning.
A 5,200 mAh battery delivers up to 300 minutes of uninterrupted cleaning.

records.recommendation_type: Best Mid-Level, Best Value, and Fan Favorite Robot Vacuum for Mid-2025
positives: 13,000 Pa turbocharged suction handles dust, debris, and pet hair on hard floors and carpets.
Multi-level LiDAR mapping enables accurate navigation around complex layouts.
The all-in-one dock automatically empties the bin, refills water and cleaning solution, washes and heat-dries mop pads, and self-cleans its washboard.
The mop lifts 10.5 mm to stay clear of carpets.
RGB camera plus 3D structured-light sensor recognizes up to 70 obstacle types even in low light.
FlexReach extending side brush reaches into corners.
Includes pet-care monitoring and “Hey MOVA” voice control.

records.recommendation_type: Best Mop System; Best for Hard Floors and Carpets; Best Overall Robot Vacuum of Mid-2025
positives: It delivers 16,600 Pa of BLAST airflow suction, among the highest measured in our tests.
The OZMO Roller self-washes to eliminate streaks.
ZeroTangle 3.0’s V-shaped roller and anti-tangle side brushes keep brushes clear.
The Triple Lift System automatically raises the mop, side brush, and main brush to adapt seamlessly between floors.
AI-driven AIVI 3D 3.0 navigation with LiDAR mapping masters layouts and avoids obstacles even in low light.
TruEdge 2.0 sensors ensure edge-to-edge coverage.
The OMNI Station washes, heats-dries, and refills for up to 150 days.
A 6,400 mAh battery powers extended cleaning sessions.
Full Alexa, Google Assistant, and Matter compatibility.
It delivered flawless obstacle avoidance in our evaluations, earning a perfect 24/24 score.
At 3.14 inches tall, its StarSight Autonomous System 2.0 combines time-of-flight sensing with an RGB camera for reliable detection and weaving around obstacles.
VertiBeam lateral-beam technology and hyper-responsive path planning ensure hesitation-free avoidance.
HyperForce suction and a zero-tangle brush roll effectively tackle debris.
Its AdaptiLift chassis crosses thresholds up to 4 cm and lifts the mop on carpets.
The 10-in-one dock automates dust collection, mop washing, heated drying, and more.
negatives: Despite a perfect lab score, its obstacle avoidance can be unreliable in real-world home environments.

records.recommendation_type: Most Innovative Robot Vacuum for Mid-2025
positives: Its OmniGrip five-axis mechanical arm autonomously stows small objects into a user-defined compartment.
Industry-leading mopping performance with a multifunction dock that washes, dries, and dispenses detergent for mop pads.
Near-flawless 3D Time-of-Flight obstacle avoidance.
Ultra-quiet cleaning mode with noise levels as low as 50 dB.
Precision edge-cleaning brushes for skirting boards.
Customizable multi-floor mapping with auto-switch profiles.
An extended 6,400 mAh battery delivers up to 300 minutes of runtime.
An AI-powered app provides real-time cleaning reports and self-updating firmware.
negatives: Its five-axis arm operated successfully in only about half of our tests.

records.recommendation_type: Runner-Up for Best Robot Vacuum for Mid-2025
positives: Its OZMO Roller system spins at 200 RPM with 16× more pressure and real-time rinsing for streak-free mopping.
TruEdge technology extends the mop for spotless corner cleaning.
An 18,000 Pa brushless motor and ZeroTangle 3.0 brushes tackle pet hair and debris on both rugs and hard floors.
AIVI 3D 3.0 with LiDAR identifies over 100 objects for intelligent navigation.
The auto-sensing LED headlight improves visibility under furniture.
Its upgraded OMNI Station washes mop pads with 167 °F water, dries them at 113 °F, empties the bin, refills water, and automatically re-mops.
negatives: It lost points for occasional stalls during our test-area navigation.
